target.name: Harbinger
target.founder: John Harris, Phillip Weicker, Will Eberts

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
Harbinger is a Series C stage transportation company founded by John Harris, Phillip Weicker, and Will Eberts. The target raised $358M to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $500M. The relevant peer universe comprises 7 private companies in Transportation, including Einride, Rivian, The Boring Company, REGENT Craft, and ZeroAvia; no public comparables exist in a sufficiently narrow peer set.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to later-stage transitions against the $500M base valuation, yielding $850M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage private comparables within the Transportation sector—only 1 same-stage peer and 0 adjacent-stage peers meet filter criteria, below the 3-datapoint threshold required for meaningful median triangulation. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable sector-specific bias.

The triangulated valuation range is $722M to $977M, with a median of $850M. This narrow range reflects dependence on a single methodology and material uncertainty around appropriate stage-progression multiples for frontier transportation assets. Key downside risks include commodity-like competitive dynamics and extended cash-burn profiles common to hardware-intensive subsectors. The analysis would be strengthened by additional Series C or post-Series C private transactions in Transportation to validate step-up multiplier assumptions.
